Name: README: Fabric Semantic Model BPA Scope and Limitations
Description: Fabric Semantic Model BPA rules: documentation, naming, UX hygiene, relationships, and measure conventions. NOTE: Direct Lake properties (EntityName, SchemaName, ExpressionSource) are not accessible via Tabular Editor BPA. Use structural validation (e.g., validate-semantic-models.ps1) to detect hardcoded OneLake GUIDs, lakehouse/workspace names, schema names, and other TMDL-level anti-patterns.

Name: [Documentation] Tables should have descriptions
Description: All tables should have a description to improve discoverability and self-service understanding.

Name: [Documentation] Measures should have descriptions
Description: Measures should have descriptions documenting business meaning, filters, and assumptions.

Name: [Documentation] Key columns should have descriptions
Description: Primary/foreign keys should have descriptions (relationships, grain, uniqueness expectations).
Expression: (Name.EndsWith("_key") or Name.EndsWith("_id") or Name.EndsWith("_ref_id")) and string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Description)

Name: [Naming] Enforce semantic layer table prefixes
Description: Semantic layer tables should follow naming conventions: dimensions start with 'dim_', facts start with 'fact_'. Allow common exceptions: bridge_, calc_, measures/_measures, and technical tables starting with '_'.
Expression: not (Name.StartsWith("dim_") or Name.StartsWith("fact_") or Name.StartsWith("bridge_") or Name.StartsWith("calc_") or Name.Equals("measures") or Name.Equals("_measures") or Name.EndsWith("_measures") or Name.StartsWith("_"))

Name: [Naming] Avoid spaces in table names
Description: Avoid spaces in table names to simplify DAX, scripting, and governance.

Name: [Naming] Avoid spaces in column names
Description: Avoid spaces in column names to simplify DAX and reduce quoting.

Name: [Naming] Avoid spaces in measure names
Description: Avoid spaces in measure names to simplify authoring and reuse in DAX.

Name: [User Experience] Hide technical/audit columns
Description: Hide common technical columns not intended for report authoring (hash/etag/rowversion/audit/ingestion/CDC metadata).
Expression: IsHidden == false and (Name.StartsWith("_") or Name.EndsWith("_hash") or Name.EndsWith("_rowhash") or Name.EndsWith("_etag") or Name.EndsWith("_rowversion") or Name.Contains("ingest") or Name.Contains("ingestion") or Name.Contains("cdc") or Name.Contains("created") or Name.Contains("updated") or Name.Contains("modified") or Name.Contains("inserted") or Name.Contains("timestamp") or Name.StartsWith("load_"))

Name: [User Experience] Foreign key columns should be hidden
Description: Hide relationship keys from the field list (except the primary key in dimension tables).
Expression: (Name.EndsWith("_key") or Name.EndsWith("_id") or Name.EndsWith("_ref_id")) and not (Table.Name.StartsWith("dim_") and Name == Table.Name.Substring(4) + "_key") and IsHidden == false

Name: [User Experience] Dimension primary keys should be visible
Description: Dimension primary key columns (dim_*.<dim>_key) should remain visible to support filtering/navigation when appropriate.
Expression: Table.Name.StartsWith("dim_") and Name.EndsWith("_key") and Name == Table.Name.Substring(4) + "_key" and IsHidden == true

Name: [User Experience] Measures table should be hidden
Description: A dedicated measures table (measures/_measures) should be hidden to keep the field list clean. Measures remain visible even if the table is hidden.

Name: [User Experience] Date dimensions should have hierarchies
Description: Date dimensions (dim_date*) should define useful hierarchies (Year > Quarter > Month > Day).

Name: [Performance] Avoid bidirectional relationships
Description: Bidirectional relationships can introduce ambiguity and can negatively affect performance. Prefer single-direction relationships. Review exceptions carefully (bridge/M2M/RLS patterns).

Name: [Performance] Avoid many-to-many relationships
Description: Many-to-many relationships often cause ambiguous results and can be slow. Prefer bridge tables with one-to-many relationships and explicit DAX patterns.

Name: [User Experience] Measures should have format strings
Description: Measures should define a format string (currency, percent, decimals, whole number) for consistent visuals and interpretation.

Name: [User Experience] Measures should be organized in display folders
Description: Use display folders to organize measures for report authors (recommended for medium/large models).

Name: [Best Practice] Measures should only be defined in fact tables
Description: Measures represent aggregations of quantitative data and should only be defined in fact tables (fact_*) or dedicated measures tables (measures/_measures). Dimension tables provide context/attributes for filtering, not calculations.
Expression: not (Table.Name.StartsWith("fact_") or Table.Name.Equals("measures") or Table.Name.Equals("_measures") or Table.Name.EndsWith("_measures"))

Name: [Hygiene] Hide unused columns
Description: Hide columns that are not used in relationships and are not referenced by other model objects. Excludes descriptive columns (*_name, *_code, *_desc, *_type, *_number), boolean columns (useful for filtering), and Data Agent metric columns (*_step, *_group, *_minutes, *_count) that may be needed for natural language queries and analysis.
Expression: IsHidden == false and !IsKey and !UsedInRelationships.Any() and !ReferencedBy.Any() and !(DataType == DataType.Boolean or Name.EndsWith("_name") or Name.EndsWith("_code") or Name.EndsWith("_desc") or Name.EndsWith("_type") or Name.EndsWith("_number") or Name.EndsWith("_step") or Name.EndsWith("_group") or Name.EndsWith("_minutes") or Name.EndsWith("_count"))

Name: [Performance] Avoid calculated columns in fact tables
Description: Calculated columns in large fact tables increase processing overhead and can complicate maintenance. Prefer measures or upstream transformations when possible.
